mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5% annually signals robust aggregate demand expansion, which lifts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-GDP-growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate dropping below 0.25% indicates a zero lower bound environment that compresses discount rates and forces portfolio rebalancing into equities, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative or flat 20-day returns in more than 40% of instances where FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25% for consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in the unemployment rate and accelerations in nonfarm payrolls confirm labor market tightening, which reduces wage-push inflation risks while supporting consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive equity returns in walk-forward testing, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index crossing above 100 signals manufacturing capacity utilization recovery, which improves corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Industrial production exceeds 100 but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of observations, indicating supply-side strength does not translate to equity gains.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts and increasing residential construction spending reflect improving consumer confidence and credit availability, which supports forward-looking equity valuations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Housing data leads SPX returns by less than 10% of instances, or the correlation between housing indicators and 5-day forward returns drops below 0.1 over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate falling by more than 0.5 percentage points within a three-month window signals rapid labor market normalization, which boosts household wealth effects and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Unemployment declines of this magnitude do not correspond to positive SPX returns in more than 45% of historical instances, or the predictive Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth accelerating alongside stable consumer price inflation indicates healthy demand-pull expansion without overheating, which sustains earnings growth expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict equity outperformance in walk-forward testing, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index exceeding 2.5% annualized while the federal funds rate remains unchanged signals an inflationary tightening cycle that increases discount rates and compresses equity valuations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Inflation spikes do not lead to negative equity returns when the Fed holds rates steady, or SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of triggered instances.
